sourceMovie.title: Southbound
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Southbound (2015) as a fantasy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The fantasy and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Look Away
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Southbound clicked for you, Look Away (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ghostland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Southbound clicked for you, Ghostland (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Southbound clicked for you, Spring (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Southbound?
faq.answer: Try Look Away, Ghostland, Spring—each shares DNA with Southbound while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Southbound?
faq.answer: Southbound (2015) is best described as Fantasy, Thriller, Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Southbound worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
